mentions.note: Valued at $800B (Dec secondary); rocket launches + Starlink satellite internet attractive; expected $22-24B revenue 2026; IPO timing/size/valuation clarity pending; hear great profitability rumors but must wait for financials.
Private company IPO expected later 2026; Space Mobile, Redwire, Rocket Lab may be proxy plays; IPO timing uncertain; investors hiding in unprofitable space names anticipating deal.
Elon Musk planning IPO by July 2026 to precede Anthropic deal; valued at $88B in recent secondary; raising capital for space-based data centers; potential mega-IPO supply risk.
Valued $1.25T post-XAI merger, seeking $1.5T for IPO later 2026. EchoStar spectrum deals make it backdoor play. Uncertain if public market will accept valuation.
5% of Fundrise Innovation Fund; aerospace/satellite play benefiting from AI infrastructure demand and Starlink expansion narrative.
Best space exposure choice for average investor; other space companies not recommended.

Targeting June IPO with $75B offering at $1.75T valuation (largest IPO ever). Red flags: reverse roadshow, Musk micromanaging allocations, non-traditional lockups allowing some insiders immediate exit post-IPO, leaving retail bagholders. Timing terrible given market weakness.

Mega IPO (~$75B, potentially $2T mkt cap) expected June 2026; Starlink, satellites, XAI subsidiary; will drain capital from Tesla and broader S&P 500; rebalancing risk if index inclusion; exciting but systemically problematic.
IPO tease mentioned (Cramer noted Verizon decline possibly connected to SpaceX IPO and Starlink competition). No direct recommendation given; pre-IPO context.
Mammoth IPO coming summer 2026; will be huge hit; better performer than Tesla long-term; space sector tailwind.
Musk planning IPO at ~$1.75-2.5T valuation. Combined with OpenAI and Anthropic, could drain liquidity from broader AI bull market.
Valuation jumped $350B → $1.25T → $2T+ in 14 months. IPO likely summer 2026. Will raise $75B+ in sliver deal. Risk: supply shock & eventual correction post-initial pop.
IPO next week valued at $1.75-2T (80x sales) on $8-18.5B revenue; loses money. Limited float could push to $5-6T valuation, creating dangerous bubble and supply shock.

IPO pricing expected ~June 11th at ~$2T valuation; will fast-track to Nasdaq-100 by ~June 22nd via new rules. Forced index buying will create enthusiasm, but Cramer concerned retail buyers could get burned on insider lockup expiration. Structural forces create uncertainty.

IPO June 12 at ~$2T market cap seems unjustifiable on Q1 2026 fundamentals (losing money, negative FCF, decelerating revenue growth). But Starship ramp, Anthropic compute deal ($1.25B/mo through May 2029), and Cursor acquisition upside could justify longer-term thesis. Set limit orders at $2T max; historically rarely wrong to bet on Musk, but stock will be expensive—don't chase IPO pop without execution proof.

IPO sliver deal (4% float) with 60% extended lockup creates favorable supply/demand mechanics; gradual float expansion through 2027 matched by index inclusion waves; Cramer targets $4 trillion market cap.
Mentioned as key Computex client; IPO expected within 2 weeks at potential $1.8T valuation. Complex lockup structure (60% locked until mid-2027) supports stock; Cramer targets $4T possible valuation.
Cramer likes Starlink's disruptive potential and Musk's execution, but capital burn is substantial and IPO retail mechanics create risk of market order losses; opening could spike to $270+.

IPO slated for $1.77T valuation at $135/share with 30% retail allocation. Long-term thesis strong (Starlink, orbital data centers) but opening vulnerable to market-order retail buyers who may get crushed if stock opens 2x+ higher.

Mega IPO driving massive cash raises by other tech firms. Won't fast-track into S&P 500 (stays under 1-year rule + profitability requirement). May not achieve profitability soon given heavy Starship/Starlink/AI infrastructure investments. Supply deluge from IPO is crushing broader market.

Bullish for multi-decade returns on space/satellite/Starship upside. Buy for long-term hold, not short-term trading. Avoid market orders at opening given oversubscription risk and potential for Cerebrus-like botch scenario.
IPO forthcoming but major concerns: likely to open at unsustainable $5T valuation due to retail market orders, then tank like Cerebrus. No clear near-term profitability. Wait for post-IPO stability.
Risk of market-order-driven opening spike (valuation $5T) followed by sharp reversal like Cerebrus deal; retail inflows may be too hot; no near-term profitability; IPO execution risk high.

IPO 4x oversubscribed but loaded with speculators/flippers; allocation mechanics more important than subscription rate. If undersized allocation, deal tight (good); if full or oversized, flippers incoming (risk). Similar to Figma/Cerebrus disasters.
IPO tomorrow with risk of uncontrolled valuation spike. Syndicate control is critical; retail demand could push stock to unsustainable levels before inevitable correction like Figma and Cerebras.

Historic IPO on 2026-06-12; betting against Musk historically terrible strategy; long-term upside 'conceivably unfathomable'; if pulls back, buy more; long-term call on space exploration and AI/robotics.

Starlink has potential to replace internet service providers globally; reusable rocket business has scarcity value; AI infrastructure rental significant ($1.2B+$920M/month). Valuation $2.6T stretched vs. Morgan Stanley but Musk's vision and multiple buyer cohorts (collectors, team members, momentum traders) keep pushing higher. Buy on thesis that long-term revenue projections justify premium.
Largest IPO in history recently completed; Cramer notes aftermath creates opportunity for sleepy interregnum in market.
